title: salix cordata var. rigida
intro: Salix cordata var. rigida, also known as the stiff-leaved willow, is a variety of the sandbar willow tree, characterized by its rigid and narrow leaves. Traditionally, the bark of Salix cordata var. rigida has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which are attributed to its salicylic acid content. The medicinal properties of this plant have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, offering relief from pain and inflammation. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, decoction, or salve, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various herbal preparations.
